description_long: This is a traditional multi-tiered pagoda structure, featuring four distinct stories with prominent, upward-curving eaves on each roof level. The roofs are clad in green tiles, each tier slightly smaller than the one below, creating a visually striking, stacked appearance. The base is constructed from stone or concrete, with a wide staircase and railings leading up to the main entrance, which is flanked by lanterns. The uppermost roof is topped with a tall, ornate finial or spire, characteristic of East Asian religious architecture. Large windows and wooden framing are visible on the main floor, while the upper levels are more enclosed. The overall design is symmetrical and highly decorative, evoking the elegance and spiritual symbolism of Japanese pagodas.
description: A four-story Japanese-style pagoda with green-tiled, upward-curving roofs, stone base, and an ornate spire. The structure features a wide staircase, wooden framing, and decorative lanterns.
description_view_0: The pagoda is seen from a rear angle, showing the staircase and railings on the right side, with the layered roofs and finial clearly visible.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the wide staircase leading directly to the main entrance, symmetrical roof tiers, and the central spire.
description_view_2: This side-rear view displays the staircase and railings on the left, with the layered roofs and lanterns visible along the facade.
description_view_3: The back view shows the stone base, symmetrical roof tiers, and the finial, with no staircase visible.
